message:
fix: raptorq recoverable decode errors, deterministic symbol ordering, and wecom download guard

Three targeted fixes in core crates and wecom connector:

fcp-raptorq/decode.rs:
- Map CorruptDecodedOutput during add_symbol() to InsufficientSymbols
  instead of InvalidSymbol — optimistic reconstruction attempts can
  fail before enough symbols arrive; this is recoverable, not a
  corruption indicator. Adds test confirming the mapping.

fcp-store/symbol_store.rs:
- Sort symbols by ESI in MemorySymbolStore::get_all_symbols() for
  deterministic ordering, since HashMap::values() iteration order is
  random. Adds test confirming sorted output.

wecom/client.rs:
- Add 50 MiB size limit for media downloads, checked both via
  Content-Length header before download and via actual byte count
  after download, preventing unbounded memory allocation from
  oversized responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
